pdf.text:
Functional  Document  
Title : Hybrid Multi -Format Document Summarization 
System Using Semantic Sentence Ranking and BART  
 
 
Kamalesh P,  Reswant Raja S  
RA23110 26010822 , RA23110 26010835  
Guide:  Dr. Sukanya Varshini K  
 
1. Introduction  
This project presents an intelligent multi -format document summarization system designed 
to automatically generate concise and meaningful summaries from heterogeneous 
document sources. The system accepts documents in formats such as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, 
images, and plain text, and pro cesses them through a hybrid summarization pipeline. The 
proposed approach combines extractive summarization using semantic sentence ranking 
with abstractive summarization using the BART model.  
The objective of the project is to reduce the time and effort required to read long 
documents, improve summary relevance through semantic ranking, and generate fluent 
summaries that preserve the important information of the original content. The system is 
intended for academic, professional, and general document summ arization applications.  
 
2. System  Overview  
User Interface (file upload + direct text input)  
• Multi -Format Text Extraction Module  
• Text Cleaning and Preprocessing Module  
• Sentence Segmentation Module  
• Semantic Embedding Module (RoBERTa -based)  
• Sentence  Scoring and Ranking Module  
• Redundancy Reduction Module  
• Adaptive Word Budget Allocation Module  
• Sentence -Aware Chunking Module  
• Abstractive Summarization Module (BART)  
• Postprocessing and Summary Output Module  
• Evaluation and Analytics Module  
The s ystem accepts input documents, extracts and preprocesses the text, identifies 
important sentences using semantic ranking, and generates a final abstractive summary. It 
also supports long -document chunking and optional evaluation using ROUGE and 
BERTScore m etrics.  
 
 3. Functional  Requirements  
The system  shall:  
     • Accept user input in the form of PDF, DOCX, PPTX, image, and pl

docx.text:
🌿 1-Page Summary: Medicinal Plants in Traditional Indian Medicine
Traditional Indian systems of medicine such as Ayurveda, Siddha, and Naturopathy rely heavily on the use of medicinal plants for maintaining health and treating diseases. These systems are based on the principle that the human body can be kept healthy by maintaining balance and harmony using natural remedies rather than synthetic drugs.
Medicinal plants like Tulsi (Ocimum sanctum), Neem (Azadirachta indica), Aloe Vera (Aloe barbadensis), Turmeric (Curcuma longa), and Amla (Phyllanthus emblica) are commonly used in daily life. Each plant has unique healing properties and specific parts such as leaves, roots, seeds, or fruits are used for treatment. For example, Tulsi is widely used for respiratory problems like cold and cough, Neem is effective in treating skin disorders, and Amla is known for boosting immunity.
The preparation methods of these plants vary depending on their use. Common traditional methods include decoction (boiling plant parts in water), paste (crushed plant material applied externally), juice extraction, and powder form. These methods are simple, cost-effective, and can be easily prepared at home.
Siddha medicine, which originated in Tamil Nadu, emphasizes the use of herbs along with minerals and metals for treating chronic diseases. Ayurveda focuses on balancing the three doshas—Vata, Pitta, and Kapha—through diet, lifestyle, and herbal remedies. Naturopathy, on the other hand, promotes healing without medicines by encouraging natural diets, fasting, yoga, and healthy living practices.
One of the major advantages of using medicinal plants is that they are easily available, affordable, and generally have fewer side effects when used properly. However, lack of proper knowledge and incorrect dosage may lead to ineffective or harmful results. Therefore, awareness and guidance are important while using these remedies.
In conclusion, medicinal plants play a vital role in traditiona
